title: Budget-aware blocker handling: fast-stop + type-conditional edit policy in implement
description:
## Problem

On 2026-06-22 GH-56 burned ~26K tokens producing a **complete** plan it already
knew would be guard-denied (a `feat` ticket whose implementation edits harness
dirs). Separately, implement on such a ticket would attempt `Write`s the guard
denies, retry, and burn the permission-denial budget — a known-failed path it is
told nothing about up front.

## Approach

- Add a rule to the read-only command files (PLAN/DECOMPOSE/REVIEW): "on a hard
  structural blocker, report `blocked` immediately — do not produce the full
  deliverable."
- Inject the guard's type-based edit policy into the **implement** prompt by ticket
  type: for a non-`system-repair` ticket, "you may not create/edit under `adw/`,
  `workflows/`, `configs/` — the guard denies it; if the plan requires that, report
  `blocked`." So implement never attempts a known-denied write.
acceptance_criteria: Plan/decompose/review stop and report `blocked` on a hard blocker without
The implement prompt states the type-conditional harness-edit prohibition; a
Full suite stays green (`uv run pytest -q`).
